prompt:
あなたはTOEIC Part 6のテスト作成専門家です。
長文穴埋め問題を作成してください。

TOEIC Part 6の形式：
- 1つの文書（メール、記事、広告など）に4つの空欄
- 各空欄に4つの選択肢（A, B, C, D）
- 語彙・文法・文脈理解を総合的に評価
- ビジネス文書形式

重要な要求事項：
1. 自然で実用的なビジネス文書を作成
2. 文法的に正確で、ネイティブスピーカーが自然に感じる表現
3. TOEIC Part 6の形式と難易度に準拠
4. 4つの空欄を適切な位置に配置
5. 各空欄に4つの選択肢を提供
6. 正解は1つのみ、他は明確に不正解
7. 日本語解説も含める

出力形式はJSON形式で以下の構造にしてください：
{
  "documentType": "指定された文書タイプ",
  "title": "文書のタイトル",
  "fullText": "空欄を(1)、(2)、(3)、(4)で示した完全な文書",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": ["選択肢A", "選択肢B", "選択肢C", "選択肢D"],
      "correct": "A|B|C|D",
      "explanation": "なぜその答えが正しいかの説明（日本語）",
      "questionType": "vocabulary|grammar|context|sentence_insertion"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "easy|medium|hard",
  "topic": "ビジネス分野",
  "wordCount": 文書の単語数
}

以下の条件でTOEIC Part 6問題を1問作成してください：

【生成条件】
- 難易度: hard
- 文書タイプ: notice (Notice/Announcement)
- ビジネストピック: cross_department_coordination (部門間連携)
- 空欄数: 4個

【特別な要求】
各空欄の正解位置は以下のように配置してください：
- 空欄1の正解位置: C
- 空欄2の正解位置: C
- 空欄3の正解位置: A
- 空欄4の正解位置: D

【問題タイプの配分】
- 空欄1: vocabulary (語彙選択)
- 空欄2: grammar (文法・語形)
- 空欄3: sentence_insertion (文挿入)
- 空欄4: context (文脈理解)

JSON形式で問題を作成してください。

{
  "documentType": "Notice",
  "title": "Interdepartmental Coordination Protocol Update",
  "fullText": "To: All Department Heads\nFrom: Office of Operations\nDate: August 18, 2025\n\nSubject: Mandatory Procedures for Cross-Department Coordination\n\nThis notice outlines revised procedures designed to improve collaboration among departments involved in the Integrated Project Initiative. Going forward, please take the following actions to ensure (1) communication across functional teams and to minimize duplication of effort.\n\n1. Weekly status reports: All teams must submit brief updates summarizing progress, blockers, and upcoming tasks. Please ensure all deliverables (2) uploaded to the shared project drive by 17:00 every Friday so that synthesis and prioritization can occur over the weekend.\n\n2. Coordination meetings: A standing meeting will take place each Monday at 10:30 to review consolidated reports and allocate resources. (3)\n\n3. Compliance and escalation: Adherence to these procedures is mandatory. Repeated failures to provide timely updates will prompt (4).\n\nIf you have questions about the procedures or require access permissions for the shared drive, contact the Coordination Office at coord-office@example.com.",
  "blanks": [
    {
      "blankId": "blank1",
      "position": 1,
      "options": [
        "A. sporadic",
        "B. exclusive",
        "C. seamless",
        "D. redundant"
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "「seamless（継ぎ目のない、途切れのない）」が最も適切です。文は「機能横断チーム間の（　　　）コミュニケーションを確保する」と言っており、スムーズで途切れのない連携を求めているため「seamless」が自然です。Aのsporadicは「断続的な」で意味が逆、Bのexclusiveは「排他的・限定的」、Dのredundantは「冗長・重複した」でどれも文脈に合いません。",
      "questionType": "vocabulary"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank2",
      "position": 2,
      "options": [
        "A. are uploaded",
        "B. were uploaded",
        "C. have been uploaded",
        "D. to be uploaded"
      ],
      "correct": "C",
      "explanation": "文脈は締切（毎週金曜17:00までに完了していること）を示しているので、完了を強調する現在完了の受動態「have been uploaded」が適切です。「Please ensure all deliverables have been uploaded by 17:00」＝締切時点でアップロード済みであることを確実にするという意味です。Aのare uploadedは命令として許容される場合もありますが、締切までに『完了している状態』を強調するには現在完了が最も適切です。Bのwere uploadedは過去形で不適切、Dのto be uploadedは不完全な不定詞構文で文法的に合いません。",
      "questionType": "grammar"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank3",
      "position": 3,
      "options": [
        "A. Each department head is responsible for consolidating reports from their teams and forwarding them to the Coordination Office by 10:00 AM every Monday.",
        "B. Please direct all questions about individual tasks to your immediate supervisor.",
        "C. Teams will no longer be required to attend the Monday meetings unless requested.",
        "D. A training session on the new software will be offered next quarter."
      ],
      "correct": "A",
      "explanation": "この挿入文は、直前の文が「毎週月曜10:30に会議を開催する」と述べているため、会議の前に『報告書を取りまとめて事前に提出する責任』について具体的に述べる文が最も自然です。Aはその役割と締切（毎週月曜10:00）を明確にし、会議の流れと整合します。Bは一般的な連絡先案内で、流れに直接つながりません。Cは会議出席に関する逆の指示で文脈と矛盾します。Dは研修の告知で、ここで挿入するには無関係です。",
      "questionType": "sentence_insertion"
    },
    {
      "blankId": "blank4",
      "position": 4,
      "options": [
        "A. temporary commendation",
        "B. departmental recognition",
        "C. an optional refresher session",
        "D. escalation to senior management"
      ],
      "correct": "D",
      "explanation": "「Repeated failures to provide timely updates will prompt (4).」という文は『繰り返しの遅延・不履行に対する対応』を述べており、最も妥当なのは重い対応である「上級管理職へのエスカレーション（escalation to senior management）」です。AやBは表彰のような肯定的措置で文脈に合わず、Cは軽微な対応であり、抑止力としては不十分です。したがってDが適切です。",
      "questionType": "context"
    }
  ],
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "topic": "cross_department_coordination (部門間連携)",
  "wordCount": 157
}

あなたはTOEIC Part 6問題の翻訳専門家です。
文書全体と選択肢を適切に日本語に翻訳してください。

重要なルール：
1. 文書は自然で分かりやすい日本語に翻訳
2. 文書の翻訳では、各空欄に正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳すること
3. 選択肢は1-4語の簡潔な日本語に翻訳
4. 選択肢に説明や解説は絶対に不要
5. 文脈に応じた最も適切な意味のみ
6. ビジネス用語は適切な日本語表現を使用

出力形式：
{
  "titleTranslation": "タイトルの翻訳",
  "contentTranslation": "文書全体の翻訳（各空欄に正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳）",
  "questionsTranslations": [
    {
      "optionTranslations": ["選択肢1", "選択肢2", "選択肢3", "選択肢4"]
    }
  ]
}

次のTOEIC Part 6問題を翻訳してください：

タイトル：Interdepartmental Coordination Protocol Update

文書全体（空欄あり）：
To: All Department Heads
From: Office of Operations
Date: August 18, 2025

Subject: Mandatory Procedures for Cross-Department Coordination

This notice outlines revised procedures designed to improve collaboration among departments involved in the Integrated Project Initiative. Going forward, please take the following actions to ensure (1) communication across functional teams and to minimize duplication of effort.

1. Weekly status reports: All teams must submit brief updates summarizing progress, blockers, and upcoming tasks. Please ensure all deliverables (2) uploaded to the shared project drive by 17:00 every Friday so that synthesis and prioritization can occur over the weekend.

2. Coordination meetings: A standing meeting will take place each Monday at 10:30 to review consolidated reports and allocate resources. (3)

3. Compliance and escalation: Adherence to these procedures is mandatory. Repeated failures to provide timely updates will prompt (4).

If you have questions about the procedures or require access permissions for the shared drive, contact the Coordination Office at coord-office@example.com.

各空欄の正解：
空欄1: C. seamless
空欄2: C. have been uploaded
空欄3: A. Each department head is responsible for consolidating reports from their teams and forwarding them to the Coordination Office by 10:00 AM every Monday.
空欄4: D. escalation to senior management

選択肢：
空欄1: A. A. sporadic, B. B. exclusive, C. C. seamless, D. D. redundant
空欄2: A. A. are uploaded, B. B. were uploaded, C. C. have been uploaded, D. D. to be uploaded
空欄3: A. A. Each department head is responsible for consolidating reports from their teams and forwarding them to the Coordination Office by 10:00 AM every Monday., B. B. Please direct all questions about individual tasks to your immediate supervisor., C. C. Teams will no longer be required to attend the Monday meetings unless requested., D. D. A training session on the new software will be offered next quarter.
空欄4: A. A. temporary commendation, B. B. departmental recognition, C. C. an optional refresher session, D. D. escalation to senior management

重要：文書の翻訳では、各空欄に上記の正解を埋めた完全な文として翻訳してください。

JSON形式で翻訳を出力してください。
